choices.text: SIGTERM to PID 1, wait grace period (default 10s), SIGKILL if still alive. App must handle SIGTERM to shut down gracefully. Configure grace with `-t SECONDS`; configure SIGTERM handler in app.

questions.prompt: JVM container with `--memory 1g` gets OOMKilled at 700MB heap. Why?

choices.text: JVM off-heap overhead (metaspace, JIT, native libs, GC) is 20-40% of total. -Xmx1g + overhead exceeds 1GB container limit. Set heap to ~70% of container memory, or use `-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=70` so JVM auto-sizes from cgroup limit.

questions.explanation: JVM heap + overhead must fit in container limit. Heap ≈ 70% of container memory.

questions.prompt: Why use `docker run --init` for many apps?

choices.text: Linux PID 1 has special semantics: must reap zombies, default signal actions don't fire. Apps not written as init programs leak zombies and ignore SIGTERM as PID 1. tini (via --init) handles these as PID 1 and execs your app as a normal child. Recommended for Node/Python/Java/Ruby apps.

questions.explanation: tini handles PID 1 responsibilities so your app doesn't have to.

questions.prompt: Docker host disk fills up after weeks. Logs are json-file. Fix?

choices.text: Add log rotation. json-file driver doesn't rotate by default — busy containers can produce GBs/day. Set `--log-opt max-size=10m --log-opt max-file=3` per container or in daemon config. Or use a shipping driver (fluentd, awslogs) that doesn't store locally.

questions.explanation: Always configure log rotation. Standard production hygiene.

questions.prompt: What makes a good HEALTHCHECK endpoint?

choices.text: Cheap, fast (<1s), verifies real readiness — process responsive plus key dependencies reachable (DB connection alive, downstream up). Returns 200 only when ready to serve traffic. Avoids heavy operations (no DB queries per check) and start-period grace handles slow startup.

questions.explanation: Healthcheck: cheap, fast, reflects real readiness.
